info.name: iFu Labs — Comply API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the iFu Labs Comply platform.

## Setup
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable to your API URL (e.g. `http://localhost:3000` for local, `https://api.ifu-labs.io` for production)
2. Set the `token` variable to a valid Auth0 JWT token
3. All requests requiring authentication include a Bearer token header automatically

## Authentication
All endpoints except `/health` require a valid Auth0 JWT in the Authorization header:
```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

## Multi-tenancy
All data is automatically scoped to the authenticated user's organisation. You cannot access another org's data.

request.description: Returns API status, timestamp, and version. No authentication required.

item.name: Auth
item.description: User authentication, onboarding, and profile management.

request.description: Returns the authenticated user and their organisation. If `onboarded: false`, redirect to onboarding. Called on every app load.

Creates organization and user account on first signup. No authentication required.

**Required fields:** name, email, password, orgName
**Optional:** orgDomain (used to auto-verify teammates with same email domain)

Returns JWT token for immediate login.
Login with email and password. Returns JWT token and sets auth cookie.
Clears the auth cookie. No authentication required.
Updates the authenticated user's display name or avatar URL.
Sends password reset email with token. Always returns success (doesn't reveal if email exists).
Resets password using token from email. Token expires after 1 hour and can only be used once.

item.name: Controls
item.description: Compliance controls across all frameworks. Controls are checked automatically by scans and stored per-organisation.

query.description: Filter by framework: soc2 | iso27001 | gdpr | hipaa | pci_dss
Filter by status: pass | fail | review | pending

request.description:
Returns all controls with their latest scan result for this org. Use `framework` and `status` query params to filter.

**Frameworks:** soc2, iso27001, gdpr, hipaa, pci_dss
**Statuses:** pass, fail, review, pending, not_applicable

Returns the compliance score broken down by framework. Used for the dashboard overview chart.

Returns `overall` score (0–100) plus per-framework breakdown with pass/fail/review/pending counts.

query.description: Number of days of history to return (default 90)
Framework to get history for: overall | soc2 | iso27001 | gdpr | hipaa | pci_dss

request.description: Returns historical compliance score data points over the specified number of days. Used for trend charts on the dashboard.

Returns a single control with its full definition, latest result, evidence detail, and last 30 scan results (history).

**Example controlIds:** CC6.1-MFA, CC7.2-CLOUDTRAIL, ISO-A.10.1.1-S3, GDPR-ART32-RDS
Adds or updates manual notes on a control result. Used to document exemptions, remediation context, or audit notes.
Returns all comments on a specific control, ordered by creation time. Used for team discussion threads on controls.
Adds a comment to a control. Used for team collaboration and audit trail on control remediation.
Edits an existing comment. Only the comment author or an admin can edit.
Deletes a comment. Only the comment author or an admin can delete.

Bulk update multiple controls at once. Assign remediation owners and update statuses in batch.

**remediationStatus:** not_started | in_progress | resolved | accepted

item.name: Integrations
item.description: Connect cloud accounts to enable automated compliance scanning. Currently supports AWS and GitHub.

request.description: Returns all integrations for this org. Credentials are never returned — only metadata and status.

Connects an AWS account via a cross-account IAM role.

**Setup steps:**
1. Customer creates an IAM role in their AWS account
2. Trust policy allows iFu Labs AWS account to assume it
3. External ID prevents confused deputy attacks
4. Attach the `SecurityAudit` managed policy
5. Submit the Role ARN here

An initial scan is queued automatically on successful connection.

Connects a GitHub organisation via GitHub App installation.

**Setup steps:**
1. Install the iFu Labs Comply GitHub App in your GitHub org
2. After installation, the URL shows: github.com/settings/installations/**12345678**
3. Submit that installation ID here

An initial scan is queued automatically.

Receives GitHub App webhook events. Configure this URL in your GitHub App settings.

Handled events: installation, push, repository, member

**Note:** Signature verification is required. The webhook secret must match `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET` in your environment.
Disconnects an integration. Scan history and control results are preserved. Requires admin role.
Queues an immediate compliance scan for the specified integration. Returns a job ID you can use to track progress via GET /scans.

item.name: Scans
item.description: Scan history and live job status.

request.description: Returns the 20 most recent scans for this org, ordered by creation time descending.
Returns a single scan with full results. If the scan is still running, also returns live progress from the BullMQ job.

item.name: Evidence
item.description: Evidence library — automated and manual evidence items. Export audit packs as PDF.

request.description: Returns all evidence items split into `automated` (from scans) and `manual` (uploaded by team). Includes total count.
Adds a manual evidence item — e.g. a policy document, vendor contract, or training record. Optionally link to a specific control result.
Removes a manual evidence item. Requires admin role. Automated evidence from scans cannot be deleted.

query.description: Framework to export: soc2 | iso27001 | gdpr | hipaa | pci_dss

request.description:
Generates and downloads a full compliance evidence pack as PDF.

The PDF includes:
- Cover page with org name, score, and date
- Table of contents
- Executive summary with pass/fail breakdown
- Per-category control sections with evidence detail
- Remediation guidance for failing controls
- Scan history

Response is a PDF binary with Content-Disposition: attachment.

item.name: Vendors
item.description: Third-party vendor risk tracking. Track SOC 2 and ISO 27001 cert expiry dates.

query.description: Filter by risk: critical | high | medium | low
Show only vendors with certs expiring within 60 days

request.description: Returns all vendors with cert status annotations (`valid`, `expiring`, `expired`, `none`) and a summary stats object.
Returns a single vendor with cert status annotations.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "AWS",
  "website": "https://aws.amazon.com",
  "category": "Infrastructure",
  "riskLevel": "critical",
  "soc2ExpiresAt": "2026-09-30",
  "iso27001ExpiresAt": "2026-12-31",
  "notes": "Primary cloud provider. BAA in place via AWS Artifact."
}

request.description:
Adds a vendor to the risk tracker.

**riskLevel:** critical | high | medium | low
**soc2ExpiresAt / iso27001ExpiresAt:** YYYY-MM-DD format. Alerts fire 60 days before expiry.
Updates any fields on a vendor. All fields are optional — only include what you want to change.
Permanently removes a vendor. Requires admin role.

item.name: AI
item.description: AI-powered compliance analysis using Claude via Amazon Bedrock.

request.description:
Generates a plain-English explanation for a failing control including:
- Summary of what's wrong
- Business impact if left unfixed
- Step-by-step fix with CLI commands
- Estimated effort
- Automation tip (Terraform/CDK snippet)

Responses are cached in Redis for 24 hours. Returns `cached: true` if served from cache.

Same as POST /ai/explain/:controlId but streams the response in real time via Server-Sent Events.

Each event is: `data: {"text": "chunk"}\n\n`
Final event is: `data: [DONE]\n\n`

Use this for the UI to show the explanation being typed out.

query.description: Framework to summarise: soc2 | iso27001 | gdpr

request.description:
Returns a high-level AI-generated compliance summary for the dashboard insight card.

Includes: headline, risk level, top priority, non-obvious insight, and 2–3 quick wins.

Cached for 6 hours.
Force-invalidates the cached AI explanation for a specific control. Call this after remediating a control so the next explanation reflects the new state.

item.name: FinOps
item.description: AWS cost optimisation — waste detection, rightsizing, Reserved Instance and Savings Plan coverage, cost trends, tag-based allocation, and AI/GPU spend analysis.

query.description: Set to true to force a fresh scan, bypassing the 6-hour cache

request.description:
Returns full FinOps findings including:
- Monthly spend and cost forecast
- Waste items (idle EBS, unused EIPs, idle NAT Gateways, idle RDS)
- Rightsizing recommendations from Cost Explorer
- Reserved Instance coverage gaps
- Savings Plan coverage gaps
- Summary with total monthly savings found

Results cached for 6 hours. Requires a connected AWS integration.

Runs a FinOps scan and streams progress updates in real time via Server-Sent Events.

Event types:
- `{type: 'status', message, progress}` — connection status
- `{type: 'progress', message, progress}` — scan progress (0–100)
- `{type: 'complete', findings}` — full findings object
- `{type: 'error', message}` — scan failed

Results are cached automatically on completion.

Lightweight summary for the dashboard card. Returns `available: false` if no FinOps scan has been run yet.

When available, returns: monthlyCost, totalMonthlySavings, totalAnnualSavings, wasteItems, rightsizingItems, coverageGaps, checkedAt.

query.description: Number of days of cost history to return (default 90)

request.description: Returns historical daily cost data points for the specified number of days. Used for cost trend charts on the FinOps dashboard.

query.description: AWS tag key to group costs by (e.g. Environment, Team, Project)

request.description: Returns cost allocation broken down by the specified AWS tag key. Useful for understanding spend by environment, team, or project.
Returns Savings Plans and Reserved Instance purchase recommendations based on historical usage patterns. Includes estimated monthly savings for each recommendation.
Returns AI and GPU spend analysis including SageMaker, Bedrock, EC2 GPU instances, and other ML/AI service costs. Highlights underutilised GPU instances and optimisation opportunities.

Exports FinOps findings in the specified format. Supports CSV, JSON, and FOCUS (FinOps Open Cost and Usage Specification) formats.

Response is a file download with appropriate Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers.
Returns the current state of all FinOps recommendations (e.g. active, snoozed, dismissed). Used to track which recommendations have been acted on.

Updates the state of a specific FinOps recommendation.

**States:** active | snoozed | dismissed
**Categories:** waste | rightsizing | coverage

When snoozed, provide `snoozedUntil` as an ISO 8601 timestamp. The recommendation will reappear after that date.

item.name: Budgets
item.description: Budget management with anomaly detection. Set monthly spend limits, configure alert thresholds, and track cost anomalies.

request.description: Returns all budgets for this org with current spend, percentage used, and alert status.

Creates a new budget.

**scope:** org | account | tag
**notifyAt:** Array of percentage thresholds that trigger email alerts (e.g. [50, 80, 100] sends alerts at 50%, 80%, and 100% of budget).
Updates an existing budget. All fields are optional — only include what you want to change.
Permanently deletes a budget and its alert history. Requires admin role.
Returns all detected cost anomalies for this org. Anomalies are flagged when daily spend deviates significantly from the rolling average.

Acknowledges or dismisses a cost anomaly.

**status:** acknowledged | dismissed

item.name: Exemptions
item.description: Control exemption requests and approvals. Request temporary exemptions from specific compliance controls with an approval workflow.

request.description: Returns all exemption requests for this org with their current approval status.

Submits a new exemption request for a specific control.

**controlId:** The control to exempt (e.g. CC6.1-MFA)
**reason:** Justification for the exemption
**expiresAt:** YYYY-MM-DD when the exemption should expire

The request enters a pending state until approved by an admin.

Approves or rejects an exemption request. Requires admin role.

**status:** approved | rejected

item.name: Audits
item.description: Audit management — track compliance audits, assign auditor firms, and export audit calendars.

request.description: Returns all audits for this org with status, framework, type, and auditor details.

Creates a new audit record.

**framework:** soc2 | iso27001 | gdpr | hipaa | pci_dss
**type:** type1 | type2 | surveillance | recertification
**expectedCloseAt:** Target completion date (YYYY-MM-DD)
**auditorFirm:** Name of the auditing firm
Updates an existing audit. All fields are optional — only include what you want to change.
Permanently deletes an audit record. Requires admin role.

Exports all audit dates as an iCal (.ics) file. Subscribe to this URL in Google Calendar, Outlook, or Apple Calendar to keep audit deadlines synced.

Response Content-Type: text/calendar

item.name: Audit Log
item.description: Immutable audit log of all actions performed in the platform. Used for compliance evidence and security investigations.

query.description: Number of entries to return (default 50, max 200)

request.description:
Returns paginated audit log entries ordered by timestamp descending. Each entry includes actor, action, resource, timestamp, and metadata.

Supports pagination via `limit` and `offset` query parameters.

Exports the full audit log as a CSV file. Response is a CSV binary with Content-Disposition: attachment.

Useful for compliance evidence packs and external SIEM ingestion.

item.name: Organizations
item.description: Organisation settings and team member management.

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's organisation details including name, slug, plan, and trial expiry.
Updates org name or domain. Requires admin role.
Returns all team members in this org. Auth0 IDs are never returned.

Changes a member's role. Requires owner role.

**Roles:** admin | member (owners cannot be changed via API)
Removes a member from the organisation. Requires owner role. Cannot remove yourself.
